title: onosma helvetica subsp. lucana
intro: Onosma helvetica subsp. lucana, also known as Lucanian Onosma, is a plant species that belongs to the borage family (Boraginaceae). It is a perennial herb that has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating skin conditions, wounds, and respiratory issues. The plant's aerial parts contain alkaloids, flavonoids, and saponins, which have shown anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, contributing to its health benefits such as wound healing, anti-aging, and protection against oxidative stress. Lucanian Onosma can be prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, often used in herbal medicine to support skin health and alleviate respiratory symptoms.
